notes.note: A house at Naples known by the name of a former owner named Rabirius; Atticus had evidently been considering it. Cicero reports its sale in case Atticus still had designs on it.
HS abbreviates the sestertius, the standard Roman unit of account. 130,000 sesterces is a modest price for a house, reflecting the bargain Cicero is reporting.
A learned companion (Cicero calls him chrēstomathēs, "fond of useful learning") staying with Quintus and Pomponia at Arpinum; otherwise little known.

notes.anchor: Our father has died on the 28th of November
notes.note: The Latin dates the death "a. d. iv Kal. Dec." --- the fourth day before the Kalends (first) of December, i.e. 28 November, by Roman inclusive counting. The deceased is M. Tullius Cicero the elder, the orator's father.

notes.anchor: any ornaments in the gymnasium style
notes.note: Cicero asks Atticus to buy Greek statuary suited to a gymnasium or palaestra (the word he uses, gymnasiōdē, means "of the gymnasium type") to decorate his villa, of a piece with the Hermathena and other pieces commissioned in Att. 1.1 and 1.4.
